NCT01740427.name: PALOMA-2

allOutcomes.title: All-cause mortality at median follow-up ~90 mo

NCT01740427.snippet: Source: Finn RS et al. NEJM 2016;375:1925-1936 (PALOMA-2); OS update Finn NEJM 2022.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Finn RS et al. NEJM 2016;375:1925-1936, CONSORT
evidence.text: 666 postmenopausal women with HR+/HER2- advanced breast cancer with no prior therapy for advanced disease were randomized 2:1 to palbociclib 125 mg + letrozole 2.5 mg (n=444) vs placebo + letrozole (n=222).

evidence.label: Primary — Progression-Free Survival
evidence.source: Finn RS et al. NEJM 2016, Figure 2
evidence.text: Median investigator-assessed PFS: 24.8 months palbociclib+letrozole vs 14.5 months placebo+letrozole. HR 0.58 (95% CI 0.46-0.72; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Overall Survival Update
evidence.source: Finn RS et al. NEJM 2022 (PALOMA-2 OS)
evidence.text: Median OS 53.9 months palbociclib-letrozole vs 51.2 months letrozole-alone; HR 0.956 (95% CI 0.777-1.177; P=0.34). OS not significantly improved despite strong PFS benefit.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Adult women with locoregionally recurrent or metastatic disease not amenable to curative therapy.~* Confirmed diagnosis of ER positive breast cancer~* No prior systemic anti-cancer therapy for advanced ER+ disease.~* Postmenopausal women~* Measurable disease as per Response Evaluation Criterion in Solid Tumors [RECIST] or bone-only disease~* Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group [ECOG] 0-2~* Adequate organ and marrow function~* Patient must agree to provide tumor tissue~E...

NCT01958021.name: MONALEESA-2
NCT01958021.snippet: Source: Hortobagyi GN et al. NEJM 2016;375:1738-1748 (MONALEESA-2); OS final Hortobagyi NEJM 2022.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 668 postmenopausal women with HR+/HER2- advanced breast cancer and no prior systemic therapy for advanced disease were randomized 1:1 to ribociclib 600 mg + letrozole 2.5 mg (n=334) vs placebo + letrozole (n=334).

evidence.label: Primary — Progression-Free Survival
evidence.text: Median central-assessed PFS: 25.3 months ribociclib+letrozole vs 16.0 months placebo+letrozole. HR 0.568 (95% CI 0.457-0.704; P<0.001).

evidence.label: Overall Survival Final (2022)
evidence.text: Median OS 63.9 months ribociclib-letrozole vs 51.4 months letrozole-alone; HR 0.76 (95% CI 0.63-0.93; P=0.004). Ribociclib is the only CDK4/6 inhibitor with significant OS benefit in 1st-line HR+/HER2- mBC.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 18 Years; max age 100 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~1. Women with advanced (locoregionally recurrent or metastatic) breast cancer that was not amenable to curative therapy.~2. The patient was postmenopausal. Postmenopausal status was defined either by:~   * Prior bilateral oophorectomy~  * Age ≥60~  * Age <60 and amenorrhea for 12 or more months (in the absence of chemotherapy, tamoxifen, toremifen, or ovarian suppression) and FSH and estradiol in the postmenopausal range per local normal range.~3. There was no prior sys...

NCT02246621.name: MONARCH-3
NCT02246621.snippet: Source: Goetz MP et al. J Clin Oncol 2017;35:3638-3646 (MONARCH-3); OS update Goetz JAMA Oncol 2024.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 493 postmenopausal women with HR+/HER2- advanced breast cancer (no prior systemic therapy for advanced disease) randomized 2:1 to abemaciclib 150 mg BID + nonsteroidal AI (anastrozole or letrozole) (n=328) vs placebo + AI (n=165).

evidence.label: Primary — Progression-Free Survival
evidence.text: Median investigator-assessed PFS at interim: not reached abemaciclib+AI vs 14.7 months placebo+AI; HR 0.540 (95% CI 0.418-0.698; P<0.001). Final median PFS 28.18 vs 14.76 months.

evidence.label: Overall Survival (final 2024)
evidence.text: Final median OS 66.8 months abemaciclib vs 53.7 months placebo; HR 0.804 (95% CI 0.637-1.015; P=0.066). Numerically favorable but did not reach statistical significance (pre-specified alpha 0.035).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: FEMALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Have a diagnosis of hormone receptor-positive (HR+), human epidermal growth factor receptor 2-negative (HER2-) breast cancer~* Have locoregionally recurrent disease not amenable to resection or radiation therapy with curative intent or metastatic disease~* Have postmenopausal status~* Have either measurable disease or nonmeasurable bone-only disease~* Have a performance status ≤1 on the Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) scale~* Have adequate organ function~* Have d...

title: RapidMeta Oncology | CDK4/6 Inhibitors in HR+/HER2- Metastatic Breast Cancer (1st Line) v1.0
